specific_concerns: The quotable line 'The artifact moved; the operator's identity did not move with it' is meme-able and could be stripped to a 'don't be attached to your code' productivity line, losing the specific four-fold compound vihāya kāmān / niḥspṛha / nirmama / nirahaṅkāra and Shankara's careful gloss. Mitigated by the analog's verbatim Sanskrit retention and the falsifiability clause that explicitly closes the misread.
verdict_reason: The engineering layer does not flatten. It preserves: (a) the four Sanskrit terms verbatim with each component named; (b) Shankara's specific gloss attributed by name; (c) the structural reading (the verse names the absence of the EGO-bond, not the absence of the work-bond); (d) the falsifiability clause that closes the abandonment misread; (e) the counter-example acknowledging that some artifacts genuinely require continued stewardship; (f) the chapter-progression context (this verse as the rung immediately below 2.72's brāhmī-sthiti) preserved. Severity 1. Pass.
